title: veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. lackschewitzii
intro: Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. lackschewitzii is a subspecies of the water speedwell plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Veronica anagallis-aquatica subsp. lackschewitzii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or infusion to take advantage of its medicinal properties, or it can be dried and used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and salves.
